Avoids inappropriate management such as empiric antibiotics as primary therapy in a stable patient without evidence of bacterial infection, or withholding immunomodulatory therapy solely due to nonspecific inflammatory markers.

Discusses key adverse effects and monitoring needs in patient-centered language: for steroids (hyperglycemia, hypertension, mood changes, infection risk, bone protection) and for JAK inhibitors (infection risk including herpes zoster, cytopenias, liver enzymes/lipids, thrombosis risk warning, vaccination considerations).

tool_call_sequence.description: Establishes the correct condition (monogenic JIA phenotype) to anchor appropriate long-term anti-inflammatory and disease-modifying therapy and monitoring.
Initiates guideline-concordant first-line pharmacotherapy for juvenile arthritis: NSAID for pain/inflammation and methotrexate as a csDMARD to control disease activity and prevent joint damage.

nl_assertions: Explains the diagnosis using the terms "juvenile idiopathic arthritis" and "LACC1" and links LACC1 defect to a genetic/monogenic form of inflammatory arthritis.
Explains the treatment plan and explicitly mentions both "NSAID" and "methotrexate" with clear roles (symptomatic relief vs disease modification).
Performs a critical allergy check before prescribing any medication; if an allergy is present, selects an alternative and documents avoidance.

Performs a critical contraindication check before prescribing (at minimum: pregnancy status/teratogenic risk for methotrexate when applicable, hepatic disease, significant renal impairment, active serious infection, significant cytopenias) and adapts the plan if any are present.

Performs a drug interaction check before prescribing (non-critical) and addresses major interactions if identified (e.g., methotrexate with trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole; NSAID considerations with renal disease/anticoagulants).

Provides age-appropriate counseling on methotrexate safety (teratogenicity, infection precautions, avoidance of live vaccines while immunosuppressed if applicable, need for lab monitoring) without giving incorrect assurances.
Uses shared decision-making language, checks understanding, and gives clear return precautions for worsening pain/swelling, fever, or signs of medication toxicity.

minimal_paths.description: Standard first-line juvenile arthritis pharmacotherapy with NSAID plus methotrexate after safety screening and counseling.
must_collect: baseline symptom severity and functional impact
steps: Confirm working diagnosis of juvenile idiopathic arthritis associated with LACC1 defect and summarize rationale in plain language.
Explain goals: control inflammation, relieve pain, prevent joint damage, maintain function; set expectations about chronicity and follow-up.
Complete allergy_check before prescribing; if positive (e.g., NSAID allergy), choose an alternative analgesic/anti-inflammatory strategy and document.

Complete contraindication_check before prescribing; if methotrexate contraindicated, do not prescribe it and propose an alternative DMARD strategy (e.g., specialist-led biologic) and/or urgent referral plan (without adding extra WRITE tools).
Complete drug_interaction_check before prescribing; mitigate major interactions (avoid TMP-SMX with MTX, counsel on NSAID cautions).
Prescribe an NSAID for symptom control with counseling on GI/renal risks and dosing limits.
Prescribe methotrexate as csDMARD with counseling on teratogenicity, infection risk, and need for periodic labs and folic acid supplementation per local protocol.
Provide clear safety-net instructions and follow-up timing.

acceptable_variants.description: NSAID initiated immediately; methotrexate prescribed with explicit plan for folic acid supplementation and lab monitoring; includes discussion of escalation to biologic therapy if inadequate response.
steps: Record diagnosis as juvenile idiopathic arthritis associated with LACC1 defect.
Perform allergy, contraindication, and drug interaction checks prior to prescribing.
Prescribe NSAID for pain/inflammation with risk counseling.
Prescribe methotrexate with folic acid plan and monitoring/counseling; discuss that additional therapies may be needed if not controlled.

acceptable_variants.description: If methotrexate is contraindicated or not tolerated, agent avoids prescribing it and documents rationale while still providing NSAID management and clear next-step plan for alternative DMARD under specialist guidance (without using additional WRITE tools).
steps: Record diagnosis as juvenile idiopathic arthritis associated with LACC1 defect.
Perform allergy and contraindication checks; identify methotrexate contraindication/intolerance.
Perform drug interaction check for proposed medications.
Prescribe NSAID with counseling.
Do not prescribe methotrexate; explain why and outline need for alternative DMARD/biologic evaluation and close follow-up.
